english: _go-dpon ('go dpon): *dpon-po-la čag ǰul dug žu zer, t'ug-sró ma kyod, ṅa yóṅ-loṅ med* (dpon po la chag jul dug zhu zer, thug sro ma kyod, nga yong long med) W. make your master my compliment, and he should not take it amiss that I had no time to come; kríms-dpon (khrims dpon) 1. prop.: superior judge, lord chief-justice. 2. now: high officer of state, prefect, = mi-dpón (mi dpon); mk'ar-dpón (mkhar dpon) commander of a fortress; k'rúg-dpon (khrug dpon) general Ma.; _gó-dpon ('go dpon) v. _go ('go); brgyá-dpon (brgya dpon) centurion, captain; bčú-dpon (bcu dpon) corporal; čibs-dpon (chibs dpon) master of the horse, equerry; rǰé-dpon (rje dpon) = rǰe (rje); rtá-pa-dpon (rta pa dpon) (sic) (cf. pa (pa) extr.) general of cavalry Glr.; stégs-dpon (stegs dpon) (?) v. stégs (stegs); stóṅ-dpon (stong dpon) leader of a thousand (seems to be no longer in use); déd-dpon (ded dpon) sea-captain; mdá-dpon (mda' dpon) is said to be in C. the modern word for general, and légs-dpon (legs dpon) the same as stóṅ-dpon (stong dpon); however v. stégs (stegs); ldíṅ-dpon (lding dpon) v. ldíṅ (lding); spyi-dpón (spyi dpon) governor general Cs.; mí-dpon (mi dpon) prefect; rtsíg-dpon (rtsig dpon) master-mason; rdzóṅ-dpon (rdzong dpon) = mk'ar-dpón (mkhar dpon); yúl-dpon (yul dpon) prefect of a district Wts.; rú-dpon (ru dpon) something like colonel; šíṅ-dpon (shing dpon) master-carpenter; slób-dpon (slob dpon) teacher, frq., also title of the higher and more learned Lamas, corresponding, as it were, to M.A., master of arts; gsól-dpon (gsol dpon) head-cook, butler. -- dpón-mo (dpon mo) fem., ṅai dpón-mo yin (ngai dpon mo yin) she is my mistress Glr. -- dpón-yod (dpon yod) standing under a master or mistress. -- dpon-méd (dpon med) free Cs. -- dpon-yyóg (dpon g-yog) master and servants, frq. -- kóṅ-ǰo dpon-yyóg (kong jo dpon g-yog) (princess) kóṅ-ǰo (kong jo) and her suite Glr. -- dpon-ts'áṅ (dpon tshang) physician Schr. and Sch. -- dpon-yíg (dpon yig) secretary Schr. -- dpon-slób (dpon slob) 1. inst. of dpón-po (dpon po) daṅ (dang) slób-ma (slob ma) Ma. and elsewh. 2. title of the four independent rulers in Bhotan, the 'Penlow' of English news-papers, acc. to the pronunciation of *pón-lob, pón-lo*.
pf. and fut. dpar (dpar), to dictate, Cs.; *por-tsóm (jhé_)-pa* C. id.

tax, duty, tribute, _búl-ba ('bul ba) to pay, Dzl., _bébs-pa ('bebs pa) to impose Tar. 21, 11; dpya-kral (dpya kral) id., rgyál-poi dpya-král (rgyal poi dpya kral) Lex.; likewise dpya-t'áṅ (dpya thang) Cs.

spyaṅ-ba (spyang ba), to suspend, to make hang down, prop. vb. a. to pyáṅ-ba (pyang ba), with pf. dpyaṅs (dpyangs) and spyaṅs (spyangs), imp. dpyaṅs (dpyangs), Sch. dpyoṅs (dpyongs), but also vb. n., to rock, to pitch (of a ship) Pth.; dpyaṅ-la gtóṅ-ba (dpyang la gtong ba) trs. Thgy.; *gyóg-čaṅ*, perh. more corr. *kyóg-čaṅ*, also *péb-čaṅ* C. sedan-chair, palanquin; dpyaṅ-t'ág (dpyang thag), pyaṅ-t'ág (pyang thag), cord or rope, by which a thing is suspended, e. g. a plummet, a bucket a miner; hence fig. t'ugs-rǰéi dpyaṅ-t'ág gčód-pa (thugs rjei dpyang thag gcod pa) Thgr.: ču-snód daṅ dpyaṅ-t'ag sbá-ba (chu snod dang dpyang thag sba ba) to hide the bucket together with the rope Schr.; a rope-swing, dpyaṅ-t'ág rtséd-pa (dpyang thag rtsed pa) to swing (one's self); dpyaṅs (dpyangs), spyaṅs-pai rgyan (spyangs pai rgyan), hanging ornaments, dar-dpyaṅs (dar dpyangs) silk ornaments S.g.
1. v. dpyód-pa (dpyod pa). - 2. Stg.: an instrument to open the mouth by force; perh. also in a more general sense: crow-bar(?); dpyád-pa (dpyad pa) v. dpyód-pa (dpyod pa).
offence, fault, blame Cs.; dpyás-čan (dpyas can) faulty, blamable; dpyas-méd (dpyas med) faultless, blameless Cs.; dpyas _dógs-pa (dpyas 'dogs pa) to blame Tar.; cf. _pyá-ba ('pya ba).
(Cs. also spyi (spyi)) W. *(s)pi*, hip Lt.; dpyi-mgó (dpyi mgo) Cs., dpyi-zúr (dpyi zur), dpyi-rús (dpyi rus), hip-bone; dpyi-míg (dpyi mig) socket of the hip-bone, perh. also vulg. = hip.
(cf. Phonetic Table), spring, also adv. in spring Dzl.; cf. also dus (dus) 4; dpyíd-ka (dpyid ka), *píd-ka* W., id., also Glr.; dpyid-zla (dpyid zla) month of spring.

dpyis (dpyis), dpyis pyín-pa (dpyis phyin pa) Sch.: to come to the last, to arrive at the end; dei rig-pa _di dpyis pyín-pa sus kyaṅ mi šes dgóṅs-nas (dei rig pa 'di dpyis phyin pa sus kyang mi shes dgongs nas) Schf.: as he reflected, that no body would thoroughly understand his arguments.
perh. primitive form of dpyaṅ-ba (dpyang ba).

pf. and fut. dpyad (dpyad), to try, to examine, nyés-pa daṅ ma-nyés-pa (nyes pa dang ma nyes pa) innocence and guilt, right and wrong Dzl.; dpyád-na . . . ma rtógs-so (dpyad na . . . ma rtogs so) after ever so much investigating . . . they found out nothing Dzl.; bye-brág-tu dpyád-pa ste (bye brag tu dpyad pa ste) having now been separately examined Zam.; sa-dpyád (sa dpyad), or ri-dpyád gzigs-pa (ri dpyad gzigs pa) to examine the country, or the mountains, i. e. their general features, with regard to omens and
